key_critique: 10/39 DSL cross-validation mismatches (25.6% failure rate) acknowledged but not statistically analyzed — since resolved to 39/39. Remaining gaps: litmus test generation is enumerative not deductive, 96-snippet benchmark lacks power analysis, theorems are paper proofs.

improvements.title: SMT-Based Litmus Test Synthesis from Scratch
improvements.description: Extend generate_discriminating_litmus_test() to actually SYNTHESIZE new litmus tests via Z3 constraint solving, not just filter existing patterns.

improvements.title: Statistical Power Analysis and Sample Size Justification
improvements.description: Add formal statistical power analysis with Clopper-Pearson exact CIs.

improvements.title: Honest Mechanization Status and Theorem Strengthening
improvements.description: Remark documenting paper proofs status; 228/228 as soundness witness.
improvements.impact: Addresses critique #7 (paper proofs not mechanized) honestly.

improvements.title: Fix C++ Atomics Accuracy (9% → 100%)
improvements.description: Fixed AST parser double-emission bug: expression_statement and call_expression/assignment_expression both emitted ops for `r0 = x.load(...)`. Added semantic analysis for C11 atomics, GCC builtins, and kernel macros (smp_store_release, READ_ONCE, WRITE_ONCE, __sync_synchronize).
improvements.impact: C++ atomics exact-match went from 1/11 (9%) to 11/11 (100%). Overall exact-match improved from 81.2% to 92.7%.

improvements.title: Build System Integration (pip + CLI + GitHub Action)
improvements.description: Created litmus-check CLI with `litmus-check --target arm src/` workflow. Updated setup.py with entry point. Created .github/workflows/litmus-check.yml GitHub Action.
improvements.impact: Provides pip-installable package with single-command workflow and CI integration.

improvements.title: Fix Theorems 1 and 3
improvements.description: Added RF×CO decomposition lemma (Lemma 1) completing Theorem 1's proof. Weakened Theorem 3 from biconditional (iff) to sufficient condition (if), with explicit note about converse failure outside 2-scope model.
improvements.impact: Makes all three paper theorems logically sound.

improvements.title: Scope Formal Claims to CPU-Only
improvements.description: Added Remark 5 (GPU formal validation scope) explicitly noting GPU models have zero SMT backing. Updated 228/228 framing throughout as 'internal consistency (CPU-only)'. Reframed all formal claims to CPU-only.
improvements.impact: Honestly addresses the GPU SMT validation gap.

improvements.title: Evaluate on Real Code (3 Projects)
improvements.description: Added 15 benchmark snippets with documented provenance from Linux kernel (5), Facebook Folly (5), and LLVM/libc++ (5). Achieves 93.3% exact-match on real-code category. Added kernel macro recognition (smp_store_release, READ_ONCE, __sync_synchronize, etc.).
improvements.impact: Demonstrates tool works on real code from major open-source projects, not just curated fragments.

improvements.title: Fix Inflated Differential Test Count
improvements.description: Separated 498 meaningful semantic checks (monotonicity, fence soundness, custom model, round-trip) from 2,280 trivial determinism checks in paper and documentation.
improvements.impact: Honest reporting of meaningful validation depth.

improvements.title: Ground Fence Costs Honestly
improvements.description: Reframed fence costs as 'analytical weight reductions' not 'performance improvements'. Added explicit disclaimer in paper that these are static cost-model estimates, not empirical speedups.

improvements.title: Reframe 228/228 as Internal Consistency
improvements.description: Changed all references from '228/228 SMT cross-validation' to '228/228 SMT internal consistency' with note that both encodings are by same authors against same specification.

expected_outcome: All improvements address the updated critiques: C++ atomics fixed (100%), build integration added, theorems corrected, GPU scoped to CPU-only, real-code evaluation on 3 projects, differential tests reported honestly, fence costs grounded, 228/228 reframed.
